summary: Tie every documentation artifact to the recognized external standard a developer already knows — never invent a house format where a SOTA standard exists.

rules.rule: Do not invent a house doc format where a widely-recognized external standard exists — a house artifact is legitimate only when no recognized external standard covers its job (Roadmap now/next/later and UPSTREAM are the only surviving ones).

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Bind each documentation artifact to its designated external standard from the canonical set — ADR to MADR, Changelog to Keep a Changelog 1.0.0, specs to SDD/SpecKit, versioning to SemVer/CalVer + Conventional Commits, PRR to Google SRE PRR, postmortems/runbooks to SRE practice, docs taxonomy to Diátaxis — using the kit-shipped templates.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Apply the supersede/rename directives: rename PRDI to PRR with sections state → gap → plan → validation → rollback, adopt the MADR template with the one ADR naming convention owned by CANON-NAMING-CONVENTIONS-001 §6, and adopt Diátaxis as the docs taxonomy.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Do not ship or mandate a work-journal artifact (BITACORA/LOG) — its content is folded into Changelog (deltas), ADR (decisions/why), and git history (chronology).

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate

rules.rule: Each consuming repo binds to the same artifact names and shipped templates (not embedded copies) and declares at L3 its docs root, ADR/decisions location and naming, adopted templates, and work-journal posture.

watch.note: declared unwatched — no gate cites this canon yet; growth candidate
